enrichment.seo_meta_description: QuestionPro is a survey and market research platform with a free plan plus paid tiers for CX, employee experience, and advanced research analytics.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud accounting software for small businesses covering invoicing, expenses, payroll, and financial reporting from Intuit.
enrichment.starting_price: From approximately $35/month (Simple Start plan, US pricing; promotions and regional pricing vary)

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: QuickBooks Online is Intuit's cloud-based accounting platform built for small and mid-sized businesses. It brings invoicing, expense tracking, bank reconciliation, and financial reporting into a single subscription-based web application, replacing the older locally installed desktop model with anytime, anywhere access from a browser or mobile device. Data syncs automatically, and feature updates roll out continuously rather than through periodic paid upgrades.

The product is owned by Intuit, a financial software company founded in 1983 and headquartered in Mountain View, California. QuickBooks Online sits inside a broader Intuit ecosystem that also includes TurboTax, Mailchimp, and Credit Karma, and it has become one of the most widely used accounting tools among small businesses and the bookkeepers and accountants who support them.

QuickBooks Online is organized into tiered monthly plans, currently Simple Start, Essentials, Plus, and Advanced, so a solo freelancer and a company with dozens of employees can each choose a feature set and price point that fits their complexity. Add-ons for payroll, payment processing, and time tracking extend the core product as a business grows.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who QuickBooks Online Is For
paragraphs: QuickBooks Online is best suited to small business owners who need real bookkeeping and financial reporting but don't want to hire a full-time accountant to run it. Freelancers, retailers, service providers, and small teams use it to send invoices, track expenses, reconcile bank accounts, and generate the profit-and-loss and balance sheet reports that lenders, tax preparers, and investors typically ask for.

It is also a strong fit for businesses that already work with an outside bookkeeper or CPA, since QuickBooks Online supports multiple user roles and gives accountants a way to log in remotely, review transactions, and make adjustments without exchanging files. Intuit's network of QuickBooks ProAdvisors means it is often the accounting system an incoming bookkeeper already knows.

Businesses that need inventory tracking, project profitability reporting, or payroll integrated with the same ledger tend to gravitate toward the higher Plus and Advanced tiers, which unlock more users, more detailed reporting, and features like batch invoicing. Companies with straightforward needs, such as a single-person consultancy, can typically operate comfortably on the entry-level Simple Start plan.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: QuickBooks Online may not be the right choice for very early-stage businesses or side projects with minimal transaction volume, since the monthly subscription cost, plus optional add-ons like payroll or payments, can feel disproportionate to a small operation's bookkeeping needs. Free or lower-cost invoicing tools may cover that use case more economically.

Larger organizations with complex multi-entity consolidation, advanced manufacturing workflows, or heavy customization requirements often outgrow QuickBooks Online and move to enterprise resource planning systems, since even the Advanced plan has practical ceilings on user counts, chart-of-accounts complexity, and industry-specific workflows compared with dedicated ERP software.

Businesses that specifically prefer owning a one-time-purchase, locally installed desktop application rather than an ongoing cloud subscription may be better served by QuickBooks Desktop or another on-premises accounting package, since QuickBooks Online requires an internet connection and recurring monthly payments rather than a single license fee.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: At its core, QuickBooks Online covers customer invoicing and payment collection, expense and receipt tracking, bank and credit card feed connections with automatic transaction categorization, and standard financial reports such as profit and loss, balance sheet, and cash flow statements. These form the backbone of day-to-day bookkeeping for most subscribers.

Beyond the basics, the platform adds mileage tracking, sales tax calculation, estimates and quotes, multiple user roles with permission controls, and, on higher tiers, inventory management and project or class-based profitability tracking. Payroll, QuickBooks Payments for card and bank transfers, and time tracking are available as integrated add-ons rather than baked into every plan.

QuickBooks Online also connects to a large marketplace of third-party apps for e-commerce, point of sale, CRM, and industry-specific workflows, and exposes an API that developers and integration platforms use to sync data with other business systems, which is a major reason accountants and software vendors build around it as a hub.

long_form_blocks.heading: The QuickBooks Online Experience
paragraphs: Day to day, QuickBooks Online presents a dashboard summarizing bank balances, outstanding invoices, and recent expenses, with navigation organized around banking, sales, expenses, and reports. New users typically spend their first sessions connecting bank feeds and categorizing a backlog of transactions, after which the software largely automates ongoing reconciliation.

The web app is complemented by iOS and Android mobile apps that let users capture receipts, send invoices, and check account balances on the go, which many small business owners rely on more than the desktop browser experience for quick tasks between client visits or jobs.

Because QuickBooks Online is accounting software rather than a simple invoicing tool, there is a learning curve for owners without bookkeeping background, and the interface can feel dense compared with lighter-weight alternatives. Most users offset this by working with an accountant who already knows the platform or by using Intuit's built-in guidance and support resources.

features.title: Invoicing and estimates
features.description: Create, send, and track customer invoices and quotes, with automatic reminders for unpaid balances.

features.title: Expense and receipt tracking
features.description: Capture receipts and categorize expenses, including automatic import from connected bank and credit card feeds.

features.title: Bank feed reconciliation
features.description: Connect financial accounts to automatically import and match transactions against the books.

features.title: Financial reporting
features.description: Generate profit and loss, balance sheet, cash flow, and other standard reports for owners, lenders, and tax preparers.

features.title: Payroll add-on
features.description: Run payroll, calculate taxes, and manage direct deposit through an integrated Intuit payroll subscription.

features.title: Multi-user roles and accountant access
features.description: Assign different permission levels to employees, bookkeepers, and accountants working in the same company file.

features.title: Mileage tracking
features.description: Automatically log business mileage via the mobile app for expense reporting and tax deductions.

features.title: Inventory management
features.description: Track quantities on hand, cost of goods sold, and reorder points for product-based businesses on eligible plans.

features.title: Project and class tracking
features.description: Track income and expenses by project, location, or class to see profitability across different parts of the business.

features.title: App marketplace and API access
features.description: Integrate with e-commerce, point-of-sale, and CRM apps, or build custom connections through Intuit's developer API.

pros: Wide adoption among small businesses and accountants, making it easy to find bookkeepers already familiar with the platform
Scales across four plan tiers, so pricing and features can grow with the business
Strong bank feed automation and standard financial reporting for owners and tax preparers
Mobile apps for iOS and Android cover invoicing, receipt capture, and mileage tracking on the go
Large ecosystem of third-party integrations and an API for connecting to other business tools
cons: Monthly subscription costs add up once payroll, payments, or higher tiers are included
Has a learning curve for users without prior bookkeeping or accounting experience
Some users report inconsistent customer support response quality
Lower-tier plans limit the number of users and access to features like inventory or project tracking
Pricing has increased over time, which has drawn criticism from long-time subscribers

faqs.question: What is the difference between QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Desktop?
faqs.answer: QuickBooks Online is a cloud-based, browser-accessible subscription product with continuous updates, while QuickBooks Desktop is locally installed software typically purchased as an annual license, often preferred by users who want offline access or more advanced industry-specific features.

faqs.question: Can I migrate existing data from QuickBooks Desktop to QuickBooks Online?
faqs.answer: Yes, Intuit provides a built-in data conversion tool to import company files, including lists, transactions, and balances, from QuickBooks Desktop into QuickBooks Online, though some complex data may require manual review after migration.

faqs.question: Does QuickBooks Online include payroll?
faqs.answer: Payroll is not included in the core plans by default; it is offered as a separate QuickBooks Payroll add-on subscription that integrates directly with the accounting data.

faqs.question: Is there a mobile app for QuickBooks Online?
faqs.answer: Yes, Intuit offers QuickBooks Online mobile apps for iOS and Android that support invoicing, expense and receipt capture, mileage tracking, and viewing account balances.

faqs.question: Can my accountant access my QuickBooks Online account?
faqs.answer: Yes, QuickBooks Online supports dedicated accountant user access, allowing an external bookkeeper or CPA to log in with their own credentials and permissions to review or adjust the books remotely.

use_cases.title: Freelancer and solopreneur invoicing
use_cases.description: A single-person consultancy uses QuickBooks Online Simple Start to send professional invoices, track client payments, and monitor basic expenses without hiring dedicated bookkeeping staff.

use_cases.title: Retail and e-commerce inventory tracking
use_cases.description: A small retailer connects QuickBooks Online to its point-of-sale or e-commerce platform to track inventory levels, cost of goods sold, and sales tax across multiple sales channels.

use_cases.title: Professional services project billing
use_cases.description: An agency or consulting firm uses class and project tracking to monitor profitability on individual client engagements and bill time and expenses accurately.
use_cases.audience: Agencies, consultancies, and professional service firms

use_cases.title: Growing business with an outside bookkeeper
use_cases.description: A company with a handful of employees uses the Plus or Advanced plan to give an external accountant restricted access, run payroll, and generate the financial reports needed for tax filing and loan applications.
use_cases.audience: Small businesses working with external accountants or bookkeepers

enrichment.seo_meta_description: QuickBooks Online: Intuit's cloud accounting software for invoicing, expenses, payroll, and reporting. Compare plans, features, pricing, and pros/cons.

enrichment.short_description: Cloud-based employee time tracking and scheduling app with GPS clock-in, built by Intuit and integrated with QuickBooks Online and Payroll.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: QuickBooks Time began life as TSheets, a time tracking company founded in 2006 in Idaho Falls, Idaho, to help hourly and field-based businesses replace paper timesheets and punch clocks. Intuit acquired TSheets in late 2017 and folded it into the QuickBooks family, rebranding it QuickBooks Time. Today it is sold both as a standalone subscription and as an add-on for QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll customers.

At its core, QuickBooks Time is a GPS-enabled mobile time clock paired with web-based scheduling and timesheet management. Employees clock in and out from a phone or tablet, their location is logged in the background while they are on the clock, and the resulting timesheets flow into payroll and job costing reports so managers can see labor costs by job, shift, or customer without re-entering hours. A companion web dashboard gives office staff and administrators a central place to review, edit, and approve those timesheets.

Because it is owned by Intuit, the product's biggest differentiator is how tightly it ties into the broader QuickBooks ecosystem: approved timesheets sync directly into QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll for running payroll and invoicing. Businesses that do not already use QuickBooks can still subscribe to it as a standalone time and scheduling tool, but the deepest value shows up for teams already inside that ecosystem.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who QuickBooks Time Is For
paragraphs: Field service and trade businesses are the clearest fit: construction crews, landscaping companies, electricians, plumbers, and cleaning services that need to verify where and when employees clocked in on a job site. GPS tracking and, on the Elite plan, geofenced reminders help owners confirm labor is billed to the right job and reduce disputes over hours worked at a site.

Shift-based employers such as restaurants, retail stores, and hospitality operators also benefit, since the scheduling tools let managers build shifts, publish schedules to the team, and get alerts when someone is approaching overtime. Multi-location businesses can manage several crews or store schedules from one dashboard rather than juggling spreadsheets or paper schedules.

Existing QuickBooks Online or QuickBooks Payroll customers, and the bookkeepers and accountants who manage their books, are a natural audience because approved timesheets flow straight into payroll runs and job costing reports without manual re-entry. For a firm already invoiced through QuickBooks, adding QuickBooks Time is often simpler than bolting on a separate, unconnected time clock system.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Businesses that have no plans to use QuickBooks for accounting or payroll lose much of the product's core appeal. Standalone time-tracking tools built independently of an accounting suite may offer comparable or deeper scheduling, HR, or project-management features without pushing customers toward a specific accounting ecosystem.

Very small teams and solopreneurs who are highly price-sensitive should weigh the cost carefully. QuickBooks Time charges a flat monthly base fee on top of a per-employee fee, and Intuit raised both tiers' per-user pricing in mid-2026, which can make it a comparatively expensive option for a one- or two-person crew versus lighter-weight time clock apps.

Organizations with a workforce that is wary of location tracking, or industries where GPS monitoring raises legal or labor-relations concerns, may also want to look elsewhere or budget time for clear policy communication. Teams that need robust project management, task boards, or detailed HR/benefits administration beyond time and attendance will likely need to pair QuickBooks Time with additional software regardless, since its feature set stays focused on time, scheduling, and payroll rather than broader workforce management.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: The centerpiece is the mobile time clock: employees clock in and out from the QuickBooks Workforce app on iPhone, iPad, or Android, and the app records GPS location in the background while they're on the clock so managers can see where work happened without asking employees to manually log a location. Timesheets can also be edited from a web dashboard for office staff or exceptions.

Scheduling tools let managers build shift or job-based schedules, drag and drop to make changes, and publish updates that notify the whole team automatically. On the Elite plan, geofencing adds automatic clock-in/clock-out reminders when an employee enters or leaves a defined job site radius, and an activity feed shows real-time status of who is working where.

Beyond the clock, the platform includes overtime and timesheet-approval alerts, PTO and time-off tracking, job/project costing reports that break down labor cost by customer or task, and direct sync with QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll so approved hours flow straight into payroll runs and invoices without duplicate data entry. Managers can also require timesheet signatures and set approval rules so hours are locked once they are reviewed, cutting down on after-the-fact edits.

long_form_blocks.heading: The QuickBooks Time Experience
paragraphs: Setup follows a familiar Intuit pattern: create an account, invite employees by email or text, and they install the QuickBooks Workforce app to start clocking in. Admins configure job codes, geofences, and approval rules from a web dashboard, and existing QuickBooks Online customers can connect the two accounts in a few clicks to start syncing timesheets.

Day to day, employees interact mostly through the mobile app, tapping to clock in, switch jobs, or request time off, while managers use the web dashboard to review timesheets, approve hours before payroll, and check schedules or the project activity feed. Notifications for overtime, missed clock-outs, and schedule changes are meant to keep both sides informed without extra follow-up calls.

Overall, the experience feels mature and reliable rather than flashy: the mobile clock-in flow is quick, GPS tracking works largely in the background, and the payroll sync is the standout convenience for QuickBooks users. The trade-off is that the interface and reporting can feel dated next to newer, purpose-built time-tracking apps, and the per-user pricing model means costs scale directly with headcount, so larger teams should budget for the recurring per-employee fee as they grow.

features.title: GPS Time Clock
features.description: Employees clock in and out from a mobile app while the app records GPS location in the background, giving managers visibility into where work happened.

features.title: Employee Scheduling
features.description: Build shift or job-based schedules, edit them with drag and drop, and publish updates that automatically notify the team.

features.title: Geofencing (Elite)
features.description: Set a virtual boundary around a job site that reminds employees to clock in or out automatically when they arrive or leave.

features.title: Overtime & Approval Alerts
features.description: Automatic alerts warn managers of approaching overtime and flag timesheets awaiting approval before payroll runs.

features.title: QuickBooks Online & Payroll Sync
features.description: Approved timesheets flow directly into QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll for running payroll and invoicing without re-entering hours.

features.title: Mobile Apps (iOS & Android)
features.description: The QuickBooks Workforce companion app lets employees clock in, view schedules, and request time off from their phone or tablet.

features.title: Job & Project Costing Reports
features.description: Break down labor hours and cost by job, customer, or project to see where time and money are actually going.

features.title: PTO & Time-Off Tracking
features.description: Employees can request paid time off through the app and managers can approve requests and track accruals alongside timesheets.

features.title: Timesheet Approvals
features.description: Managers review and approve or reject submitted hours from a web dashboard before they are sent to payroll.

features.title: Project Activity Feed (Elite)
features.description: A real-time feed shows which employees are currently clocked in and where, useful for overseeing multiple active job sites at once.

pros: Deep, native integration with QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll for payroll and invoicing
GPS-based time tracking helps verify job-site hours and reduce time theft
Mobile apps for iOS and Android make clock-in simple for field and shift workers
Scheduling tools with automatic notifications reduce back-and-forth over shift changes
Established, mature product backed by Intuit with a long track record since its TSheets origins
cons: Per-user pricing on top of a monthly base fee can get expensive as headcount grows
Geofencing and the project activity feed are limited to the pricier Elite tier
Value is strongest for existing QuickBooks users; standalone users may find less benefit
GPS/location tracking can raise employee privacy concerns if not communicated clearly
Recent price increases have made per-user costs less competitive against some standalone time-tracking apps

faqs.question: What is QuickBooks Time?
faqs.answer: QuickBooks Time, formerly TSheets, is Intuit's cloud-based employee time tracking and scheduling app that uses a GPS-enabled mobile time clock and syncs approved timesheets into QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll.

faqs.question: Do I need QuickBooks Online to use QuickBooks Time?
faqs.answer: No, it can be purchased as a standalone subscription, but it offers its deepest value when connected to a QuickBooks Online or QuickBooks Payroll account for automatic timesheet-to-payroll syncing.

faqs.question: What is the difference between the Premium and Elite plans?
faqs.answer: Both plans include GPS time tracking, scheduling, and QuickBooks integration; Elite adds geofencing, a project activity feed, and additional oversight tools for a higher base fee and the same per-user rate.

faqs.question: Does QuickBooks Time track employees when they are off the clock?
faqs.answer: The mobile app is designed to log location primarily while an employee is clocked in; geofencing features check location to detect entry or exit from a job site to trigger reminders.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial for QuickBooks Time?
faqs.answer: Intuit has historically offered a free trial period for QuickBooks Time so businesses can test the time clock and scheduling features before committing to a paid subscription.

use_cases.title: GPS-Verified Field Service Time Tracking
use_cases.description: Construction, landscaping, and home-service crews clock in from job sites so owners can confirm labor hours match the work location and bill customers accurately.
use_cases.audience: Construction and field service business owners

use_cases.title: Shift Scheduling for Retail and Restaurants
use_cases.description: Managers build and publish shift schedules, track attendance, and get overtime alerts across one or more store or restaurant locations.

use_cases.title: Payroll-Integrated Timesheet Approval
use_cases.description: Bookkeepers and accounting teams approve employee timesheets that sync directly into QuickBooks Online and QuickBooks Payroll for faster, error-free payroll runs.

use_cases.title: Multi-Site Labor Cost Tracking
use_cases.description: Businesses running several job sites or locations use project costing reports to see labor cost broken down by job, customer, or crew.
use_cases.audience: Operations managers overseeing multiple job sites

features: Mobile apps for iOS and Android

enrichment.seo_meta_description: QuickBooks Time review: GPS time tracking, scheduling, and payroll sync built by Intuit. See pricing, features, pros and cons before you subscribe.

enrichment.short_description: AI writing assistant for paraphrasing, grammar checking, summarizing, and citation generation for students and professionals.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: QuillBot is an AI-powered writing assistant built around paraphrasing, grammar correction, and a set of adjacent writing tools such as a summarizer, citation generator, plagiarism checker, and AI content detector. It was founded in 2017 by a small team of computer science students and grew quickly on the strength of its paraphrasing engine, which rewrites sentences while preserving meaning and letting users dial in a preferred tone or level of formality.

In 2021, QuillBot was acquired by Course Hero, which later rebranded its parent company as Learneo, a group that also owns Scribbr, CliffsNotes, LanguageTool, and Symbolab. Under Learneo, QuillBot expanded from a single paraphrasing web tool into a broader writing suite available through its website, a Chrome browser extension, a Microsoft Word add-in, and mobile keyboard apps for iOS and Android, all tied to one subscription.

The product sits in the same general category as Grammarly and ProWritingAid but is best known specifically for paraphrasing quality and academic-adjacent tools like citation formatting and plagiarism checking. Its freemium model gives casual users a genuinely usable free tier, while QuillBot Premium unlocks longer inputs, additional paraphrasing modes, and unlimited use of the grammar and other writing tools.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who QuillBot Is For
paragraphs: QuillBot is a strong fit for students and academic writers who need to rephrase source material, format citations, and check drafts for grammar and unintentional plagiarism before submission. Its citation generator and plagiarism checker are tailored to essay and research-paper workflows, and the paraphraser's tone and mode controls help with rewriting dense academic language into clearer sentences.

It also appeals to non-native English speakers and ESL learners who want quick, low-friction help rephrasing sentences and catching grammar mistakes without the more complex tone-and-style tooling found in enterprise writing suites. Professionals writing routine emails, internal documents, or short marketing copy can similarly use it as a fast rewriting and proofreading layer inside their existing apps via the browser extension and Word add-in.

Freelance writers, bloggers, and small content teams who need occasional rewriting and summarizing support, rather than a full brand-voice or long-form generation platform, are a good match too. Because the free plan is usable on its own, casual or infrequent users can rely on QuillBot without committing to a paid subscription.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who Should Avoid It
paragraphs: Teams that need enterprise-grade style guides, brand voice enforcement, or large-scale long-form AI content generation are usually better served by dedicated content platforms or Grammarly Business, since QuillBot's feature set is centered on rewriting and proofreading rather than generating full articles from scratch at scale.

Students or professionals who need to demonstrate strict academic integrity should be cautious about leaning on paraphrasing tools as a substitute for original writing; some institutions flag heavily AI-rephrased text, so QuillBot works best as an editing aid rather than a way to disguise unoriginal work.

Organizations that require extensive admin controls, single sign-on, granular compliance settings, or large multi-department rollouts may also find QuillBot's Teams offering limited compared to purpose-built enterprise writing or security platforms, since it is designed for small groups rather than large-scale corporate deployment.

long_form_blocks.heading: Main Features
paragraphs: The core of QuillBot is its Paraphraser, which offers multiple rewriting modes (such as Standard, Fluency, Formal, and Creative) plus a synonym slider to control how aggressively wording changes, alongside a Grammar Checker that flags spelling, punctuation, and grammar issues with suggested fixes.

Beyond rewriting, QuillBot includes a Summarizer that condenses long text into key points or paragraph summaries, a Citation Generator that formats references in styles like APA, MLA, and Chicago, and a Plagiarism Checker that compares submitted text against web and academic sources to flag overlap.

Rounding out the suite are an AI Content Detector for estimating whether text was AI-generated, a Co-Writer workspace for drafting and organizing longer documents, a Translator supporting dozens of languages, and distribution through a Chrome extension, Microsoft Word add-in, and mobile keyboard apps so the tools travel with the user across platforms.

long_form_blocks.heading: The QuillBot Experience
paragraphs: Day to day, QuillBot presents a clean, minimal interface: paste text into the Paraphraser or Grammar Checker and see suggestions appear inline, with the Chrome extension and Word add-in surfacing the same corrections directly inside Gmail, Google Docs, LinkedIn, or Word documents without switching tabs.

The free plan covers shorter inputs and a limited set of paraphrasing modes, which is enough for occasional use, while Premium removes those caps, unlocks every paraphrasing mode, and adds unlimited use of the grammar, summarizing, and other writing tools, along with faster processing on longer passages.

Overall, the experience favors speed and simplicity over deep customization: there is little setup required, the learning curve is shallow, and most users are productive within minutes, though writers wanting fine-grained brand-voice control or long-form drafting from a blank page may find the toolset narrower than an all-in-one AI writing platform.

features.title: Paraphraser
features.description: Rewrites sentences in multiple modes (Standard, Fluency, Formal, Creative, and more) with a synonym slider to control how much wording changes.

features.title: Grammar Checker
features.description: Flags spelling, punctuation, and grammar errors and suggests corrections as you write.

features.title: Plagiarism Checker
features.description: Scans submitted text against web and academic sources to highlight matching or unoriginal passages.

features.title: Summarizer
features.description: Condenses long articles or documents into key-point or paragraph-style summaries.

features.title: Citation Generator
features.description: Formats references and bibliographies automatically in styles such as APA, MLA, and Chicago.

features.title: AI Content Detector
features.description: Estimates the likelihood that a piece of text was generated by AI.

features.title: Co-Writer
features.description: A document workspace for drafting, organizing, and editing longer pieces of writing in one place.

features.title: Translator
features.description: Translates text across dozens of languages while aiming to preserve tone and meaning.

features.title: Browser and Word Extensions
features.description: Chrome extension and Microsoft Word add-in bring grammar and paraphrasing suggestions directly into other apps.

features.title: Mobile Keyboard Apps
features.description: iOS and Android keyboard apps apply paraphrasing and grammar checks while typing on mobile devices.

pros: Free plan is genuinely usable, not just a limited demo
Paraphraser offers multiple tone and style modes with fine control over rewrite intensity
Works across the web app, Chrome extension, Word add-in, and mobile keyboard under one subscription
Bundles grammar, summarizing, citation, and plagiarism tools alongside paraphrasing
Simple, low-friction interface with a short learning curve
cons: Free tier limits input length and available paraphrasing modes
Heavy paraphrasing can alter nuance or meaning in technical or specialized writing
Not designed for generating long-form content from scratch like dedicated AI writing platforms
Overreliance on paraphrasing may raise academic integrity concerns in some institutions
Teams plan offers fewer enterprise admin and compliance controls than larger writing suites

faqs.question: Is QuillBot free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, QuillBot offers a free plan with basic paraphrasing, grammar checking, and other tools, though with limits on input length and available modes. A paid Premium plan removes those limits and unlocks additional features.

faqs.question: Who owns QuillBot?
faqs.answer: QuillBot was founded in 2017 and acquired by Course Hero in 2021. Course Hero later rebranded as Learneo, which also owns Scribbr, CliffsNotes, LanguageTool, and Symbolab.

faqs.question: Does QuillBot have a plagiarism checker?
faqs.answer: Yes, QuillBot includes a Plagiarism Checker that compares submitted text against web and academic sources to flag overlapping or unoriginal content.

faqs.question: Can QuillBot be used on mobile devices?
faqs.answer: Yes, QuillBot offers keyboard apps for iOS and Android that apply paraphrasing and grammar suggestions while typing, in addition to the web app and browser extension.

faqs.question: Is QuillBot's paraphrased text detectable as AI-written?
faqs.answer: It can be, depending on the AI detection tool and how heavily the text is rewritten. QuillBot also offers its own AI Content Detector, but users should treat paraphrasing as an editing aid rather than a guaranteed way to avoid detection.

use_cases.title: Academic essay revision
use_cases.description: Rephrasing source material, formatting citations, and running a plagiarism check before submitting coursework.

use_cases.title: Grammar and tone cleanup for professional emails
use_cases.description: Using the Chrome extension or Word add-in to catch grammar mistakes and adjust tone in daily business writing.

use_cases.title: Content rewriting for blogs and marketing copy
use_cases.description: Rephrasing drafts to vary wording and improve readability before publishing.

use_cases.title: Writing support for non-native English speakers
use_cases.description: Getting quick grammar corrections and clearer phrasing suggestions while composing English text.
use_cases.audience: ESL learners and non-native English speakers

features: Access to web app, extension, and mobile keyboard
Access to Summarizer, Citation Generator, and Plagiarism Checker
Citation Generator and Plagiarism Checker included

enrichment.seo_meta_description: QuillBot review: AI paraphraser, grammar checker, summarizer, and citation tool with a free plan and Premium subscription.
